task_completion.description: Rocket.Chat is open in the Firefox browser
task_completion.visual_evidence: Firefox browser window is visible and displaying the Rocket.Chat web interface.

task_completion.description: A public channel named 'glossary' is created and active
task_completion.visual_evidence: The left sidebar of Rocket.Chat shows a channel named 'glossary' (typically with a '#' icon indicating a public channel), and it is highlighted as the currently active chat.

task_completion.description: A message containing a formatted table is posted in the channel
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main chat area of the 'glossary' channel contains a message sent by the admin that is rendered as a table.

task_completion.description: The table has the correct header row
task_completion.visual_evidence: The top row of the posted table contains exactly two column headers: 'Acronym' and 'Definition'.

task_completion.description: The table contains the correct acronyms and definitions from the CSV
task_completion.visual_evidence: The table rows contain the terms API, JSON, RBAC, and VPN, paired with their exact definitions (Application Programming Interface, JavaScript Object Notation, Role-Based Access Control, Virtual Private Network).

integrity.description: The agent used the Firefox GUI to interact with Rocket.Chat
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots should show the Firefox browser being used to navigate Rocket.Chat. There should be no terminal windows showing curl commands or Python scripts used to bypass the UI and interact with the Rocket.Chat API directly.

integrity.description: The table is posted in the actual Rocket.Chat application
integrity.visual_evidence: The table must be visible inside the Rocket.Chat message history area, complete with chat UI elements (message input box, sidebar, user avatars), rather than just being typed into a text editor or markdown previewer.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply create a fake HTML page to mimic Rocket.Chat
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser address bar should show the local Rocket.Chat URL (typically localhost or a local IP on port 3000), not a file:// URI.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target channel name: 'glossary'. Expected table contents: API (Application Programming Interface), JSON (JavaScript Object Notation), RBAC (Role-Based Access Control), VPN (Virtual Private Network).
